# Extração: 05-The BPM Method - H-Scale Split

- **Fonte:** 05-The BPM Method - H-Scale Split.docx
- **Tipo:** DOCX
- **Tamanho:** 117.0 KB

---

H-Scale Split



Use this alongside the training video to implement this strategy.



Use H-Scale on completely unique paths for example if your audience is 25-50 generally yet 25-34 consistently performs well, then I’d say with high confidence pulling out 25-34 year olds is safe and a good decision to push more spend here, depending on audience size and not let Facebook spread the budget to audiences that don’t work.



The same applies to Gender, Country, Region - people don’t change these during the day in general!

However with Placement and Device, these are interchangeable during the day, so they’re the last things I would H-Scale into.

When H-Scaling there are a few primary scenarios:

Gender: you can run them in the same CBO, split into M & F depending on number of ad sets (I’m trying to keep them up to 7, ideally 5) though you can split them into separate CBOs (my preference as explained earlier)

Age: as above though in this case depending on performance and splits I end up with I may split into their own CBOs

Country: generally as this is a higher level ‘split’ I’d always put my top countries into their own separate CBOs and then group countries where I have smaller volume and spend

Region: I’d generally include into the same CBO grouped by country - it’s not often I split this especially if I want to scale

Now, when splitting by Placement and Device it gets more complicated - my advice here is, where possible, to not split these out for scaling as it’ll come back to bite you later if your attributions don’t align - one example of splitting is taking your single best audience and splitting by Placement and Device into a single CBO (so you split a SINGLE audience, into separate ad sets split by: Facebook, Instagram, Mobile, Desktop), with the theory CBO does the same thing of putting them all back into the auction but giving you more flexibility. This can work but again for scaling I prefer fewer ad sets and giving larger audiences in each ad set. 1 million audience sizes are great, 10 million even better. More than that is perfect.





Note: Duplicating winning ad sets is working far less than it used to pre-2021, so use with caution! Especially as it can cause instability with the already well performing campaign(s). You’ve been warned!
